text: Last class you said what each party must, shall or may do.
Today you say what you will do if a party breaks a duty.
Dispute
Mediation
Arbitration
Claim
Settle
Award
Binding
Resolve
If the parties cannot agree, we will try mediation.
If mediation fails, we will go to arbitration.
We will save money if we settle the claim early.
If the arbitrator decides, the award will be binding.
Thank you for the call. We know there is a dispute about the last payment. If we work together, we will resolve it quickly.
I agree. We have a claim for the delay, but we prefer to talk. If you accept mediation, we will not start a court case.
We accept mediation. If we settle the claim there, we will both save time. If mediation fails, we will go to arbitration, as the clause says.
Good. And remember, if the arbitrator decides, the award will be binding. So mediation is better for both of us.
Exactly. If we resolve the dispute in mediation, we keep a good relationship. Let us choose a mediator this week.
Perfect. If you send me three names today, I will confirm one by Friday. I think we will settle this without arbitration.
We want to resolve the dispute quickly.

Hi Simone, this is Daniel from the legal team. We have a problem with the German partner. They say we did not meet a deadline, and now there is a dispute about the last payment. I want your help before this gets worse. First, please read the dispute clause in the contract. It says that if the parties cannot agree, they will try mediation, and if mediation fails, they will go to arbitration in London. I do not want a court case, so mediation is a good first step. If we settle the claim early, we will save time and money. But if the partner refuses to talk, we will have to start arbitration, and the award will be binding on both sides. Please check whether we can appeal an arbitration award under this contract. If we cannot, we must be very careful. Let us talk on Thursday. If you have any questions before then, just call me. Thank you, and talk soon.

Daniel tells Simone that there is a dispute with the German partner about the last payment. Simone reads the dispute clause and sees that the parties will try mediation first. She suggests they settle the claim early to save time and money. The partner refuses to talk, so the parties start arbitration in London. The arbitrator gives a binding award, and both sides accept the decision.
